source_paper_title: On Time-Consistent Equilibrium Stopping Under Aggregation of Diverse Discount Rates

Section 5.1, after Example 2 (around p. 28 in the provided parse)

As a sharp contrast, in our framework with a general attitude function

title: General characterization of the optimal equilibrium under nonlinear aggregation attitude
latex:
Given a time-inconsistent optimal stopping problem under aggregation of diverse discount rates, let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,(\mathcal F_t)_{t\ge0},\mathbb P)\) support a one-dimensional time-homogeneous diffusion \(X^x=(X^x_t)_{t\ge0}\) with \(X^x_0=x\in\mathbb R\), and let \(\rho\) be an \([0,\infty)\)-valued random variable independent of \(X^x\) with distribution function \(F_\rho\). Fix a Borel measurable payoff \(g:\mathbb R\to\mathbb R\) and a strictly increasing aggregation attitude function \(\phi:\mathbb R\to\mathbb R\). For any Borel set \(R\subseteq\mathbb R\), define the hitting time \(\tau(x,R):=\inf\{t>0: X^x_t\in R\}\) and the aggregated continuation value
\[
J(x,R):=\int_0^{\infty} \phi\Big(\mathbb E\big[e^{-r\tau(x,R)}\,g(X^x_{\tau(x,R)})\big]\Big)\,dF_\rho(r).
\]
Define the policy improvement operator \(\Theta\) by
\[
\Theta(R):=R\cup\{x\in\mathbb R: \phi(g(x))>J(x,R)\},
\]
and call \(R\) a (mild) equilibrium if \(\Theta(R)=R\). Let \(\mathcal E\) be the set of all equilibria. For any \(R\in\mathcal E\), define its induced equilibrium value
\[
V(x,R):=\max\{\phi(g(x)),\,J(x,R)\},\qquad x\in\mathbb R.
\]
An equilibrium \(R^\star\in\mathcal E\) is called an optimal equilibrium if
\[
V(x,R^\star)\ge V(x,R)\quad\text{for all }x\in\mathbb R\text{ and all }R\in\mathcal E.
\]
Problem: Under general choices of \(\phi\) (in particular when \(\phi\) does not satisfy the paper’s sufficient condition C-(iii)) and general diffusion/payoff specifications for which equilibria may not be totally ordered by set inclusion, give conditions under which an optimal equilibrium exists, and if it exists, provide a general characterization (e.g., in terms of \(\Theta\), set operations on \(\mathcal E\), or an analytic characterization such as an extended HJB/free-boundary condition) of such an optimal equilibrium \(R^\star\).
